auto_context.text:
Read docs/ai/code-summary.md and docs/ai/requirements-summary.md if you haven't yet or if you feel you might be out-of-date.

Add a new requirements document.

Ask clarifying questions as needed.

Be sure to create follow up tickets as needed as well.

Read docs/ai/code-summary.md and docs/ai/requirements-summary.md if you haven't yet or if you feel you might be out-of-date.

Update requirements documents as appropriate.

Ask clarifying questions as needed.

Read docs/ai/code-summary.md and docs/ai/requirements-summary.md if you haven't yet or if you feel you might be out-of-date.

Evaluate current tests and add unit tests or test automation to protected against this kind of bug going forward, if applicable.

categories.label: Bug
categories.description: Bugs that should be fixed in the codebase

custom_commands.name: Commit Changes
custom_commands.prompt: make a commit message for the recently completed tickets, without wrapping long lines.  Don't include any co-authored by Anthropic type lines in the commit message.  add all unstaged changes to the git commit.  git commit with the message you generated but don't push

custom_commands.name: Update Requirements
custom_commands.prompt: Update the requirements docs as appropriate

children.name: Everything
children.prompt:
do these sequentially, creating follow up tickets for all concerns encountered:
/check-requirements-against-code
/check-code-hygiene
/analyze-code-quality

children.name: Kerf Usage Audit
children.prompt: Audit this codebase for kerf usage.  See ~/.claude/skills/kerf-app/SKILL.md or node_modules/kerfjs/ai/skill.md.  For each finding, make Hot Sheet tickets with: file:line, which rule or pattern, the fix shape. Don't speculate - only flag patterns you can point to in the code. Top "must fix" list, then "consider" list.
